original: textify(a||(a=(0,g.Z)(["The Ova’LEK’s front-mounted boarding ramp joins against its finely machined hull with molecular precision. No doubt an engineering decision made to reduce the vessel’s signature profile, it is but one of a great many such innovations. Another is the vibration-dampening foam that covers many of the Ova’LEK’s internals - something you’ll be grateful for the first time you smack your head into a squishy bulkhead. The helm is a short jaunt to the south from here. Swift egress is available via the boarding ramp."])))

textify(n||(n=(0,g.Z)(["Unlike many other vessels, the Ova’LEK doesn’t come standard with cockpit windows of any design. Instead it maintains its stealthy profile with two recessed sensor housings, creating a three-dimensional holographic projection around the pilot. Included gloves grant the pilot the ability to directly interact with the environmental display and the included data laid overtop of the view.

		Boarding access is to the north.
		
		The security console is accessible to the south, as well as the rest of the ship."])))

textify(i||(i=(0,g.Z)(["A recessed security panel bulges forth from the smoothly machined lining of the Ova’LEK’s wall, its display as bright and crisp as the stars it was meant to fly between. Ovir engineers apparently favor the clarity of pure design over the accessibility of easy repair, and you can see reflections of that philosophy everywhere from the shape of the vessel itself to the seamlessly meshed floorplates under[pc.foot]. You can find the hold toward the aft of the ship, along its magnetic south, while the cockpit and boarding ramp both lie ‘north.’\n\n\t\tThe security console affords you easy access to any crew members from this locale. The crew quarters themselves lie below, through a compact lift into the belly of the ship. You have no reason to go there yourself."])))

textify(s||(s=(0,g.Z)(["Mechanized intake and outtake ports segment you from the hold proper, trusting the arrangement and storage of any onboard cargo to the cold precision of a calculating V.I. over the imperfect intuition of any organic crew. Morphic geometry around the ports allows them to adjust to near any size (within the confines of the ship itself.) There isn’t even a maintenance override! In the event of a fault or damage, you’ll need to take it back to port... or find someone willing to crawl through the intake and into its guts. At least the centralized location should protect it from damage in a firefight.

		Captain’s quarters lie through a reinforced door to the west while a space for user-installed equipment sits to the east.
		
		You can access your ship’s storage from this location."])))

textify(r||(r=(0,g.Z)(['The Captain’s quarters of Ova’LEK class vessels come designed with Ovir comforts in mind. The bed is memory foam - with a removable insert to allow tail-bearing officers to sleep in supine comfort, should they desire. A self-cleaning trough around it collects shed scales (or spilled fluids!) with ease, and a three dimensional hardlight interface along the wall gives full control over the local atmospheric climate. The default appears to be set quite dry.

		There is even a walk-in shower, though it was designed with a "sonic" setting in mind. "H2O" appears to have been added as an afterthought.
		
		Hold access is available to the east, just past the reinforced door.'])))

textify(u||(u=(0,g.Z)(["The smoother curves presented elsewhere are nowhere to be found in this small, square space. Instead, U.G.C.-standardized fittings and sockets are the order of the day, no doubt arranged this way to accept military-standardized fittings for advanced 3D printers, weapon maintenance stations, and anything else a stealth operative on the go might need to slot in. Fortunately, most ship-installable equipment in the galaxy is designed to work in such an environment.\n\n\t\tThe Ova’LEK’s storage hold is accessible to your west. The Captain’s quarters lie a few paces beyond. Housings for the sunlight engines, the light drive, and the ship’s power system occupy the aft, just south of here."])))

textify(l||(l=(0,g.Z)(["The port engines appear to have completely consumed the western wall, replacing engineered smoothness with rounded organ-like bulk. Layer after layer of EM-absorbent material ensures that standard engines and light drives both have the smallest possible signal profile.\n\n\t\tYou can visit the reactor the east."])))

textify(h||(h=(0,g.Z)(["Ova’LEK reactors, like the ship itself, are designed to be as stealthy as the ship itself, and that means draping it even more EM-dampening shielding than the engines themselves. Conduits for coolants and power output jut from it like arteries from a heart, pumping electrical lifeblood through the ship.\n\n\t\tUnsurprisingly, the starboard engines are a few paces to the east, mirrored identically to west. A cramped hatch to the north provides quick exit to the hold in case of emergency."])))

textify(c||(c=(0,g.Z)(["Housings for the starboard drive and light-drive pairing stand in place of an eastern wall, shrouded by the thick, electromagnetic shielding. Similarly built-up conduits appear as veiny bulges in the ceiling, feeding power from the central reactor to these technologically-muzzled beasts.\n\n\t\tThe reactor is visible to the west. A space for installed equipment lies northward."])))

textify(d||(d=(0,g.Z)(["No amount of shielding inside the ship can conceal the energy pouring out of wide-open nozzles in flight. Yet somehow this module comes close. Like the noise-cancelling headphones of old, this compartment produces inverted electromagnetic waves to cancel out the worst of its propulsive emissions.\n\n\t\tYou can return to the reactor to the north."])))
